screens.name: Browser-first visual workflow
screens.purpose: Ensure visual UI changes are checked in the browser before edits and after edits instead of relying on code-only judgment.
screens.primary_action: Classify a task as visual UI work and require browser evidence

ux_rules: Treat styling, spacing, typography, layout, and other visual UI changes as browser-verified work by default.
For visual tasks, inspect the existing UI before editing so issues are identified from the rendered surface rather than inferred from code alone.
For visual tasks, capture after-change browser evidence before verification and review are considered complete.
Keep the policy explicit enough that agents can tell when browser checks are required and what evidence must be recorded.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Represent browser verification as a standard workflow requirement for visual tasks, not as an optional polish step.
interaction_risks: Agents may still skip browser checks if the workflow language stays advisory rather than mandatory.
A vague definition of visual work could make the policy inconsistent across runs.
notes: This run targets workflow behavior and documentation, not a user-facing product surface.

constraints: Update the main operating manual and the primary workflow docs in the same pass so agents do not see conflicting guidance.
Make browser checks mandatory language for visual UI work rather than a soft recommendation.
Record any remaining non-enforced gaps explicitly if the rule is not backed by supervisor runtime code.
open_decisions: Whether the supervisor should later hard-fail visual runs that lack screenshot evidence remains open.

critical_judgments: The updated workflow should make browser-first verification obvious for visual tasks.
The rule should be specific enough to follow without applying to unrelated non-visual work.

visual_constraints: Prefer operational clarity over vague reminders.
State the before-edit and after-edit browser checkpoints explicitly.
Tie browser verification to screenshot-backed evidence wherever review artifacts already support it.
